posts.text: The model didn't love that narrow Duke escape, not a huge swing in the odds, but their championship probability is down a hair. Nice wins by Houston and Illinois.

Would have been the biggest upset ever btw. We actually have Norfolk St. vs. Missouri (~2%) in 2012 ahead of the two 16-1 upsets as the least likely historical NCAA outcome, but Duke-Siena would have been a 1% chance.
If a #1 seed falls 10 points behind any school that runs a polling institute at any point in the game (Siena, Quinnipiac, Marist) they should just be eliminated IMO.
37 minutes to go and I'm still working on mine lol

Judging by longstanding Silver Bulletin/538 traffic patterns the peak time for people filling out NCAA brackets is basically 10pm the night before (i.e. right now). And then like literally 5 minutes before the deadline on Thursday AM. As a fellow procrastinator I admire it.
natesilver.net/p/2026-womens…
Final women's NCAA projections before First Four games. UConn with a pretty clear lead, no surprise, though or model likes the other #1 seeds a bit more than consensus.

The point is that 26% is 1 in 4 voters which is ok but not great when you’re the insurgent candidate running against two establishment choices. She would have lost by more in a 2-way race.


Annie Wu (all socials: @annie_wu_22) (@Annie_Wu_22)

26-29 (the result) is actually a smaller margin than 46-50 (his hypothetical)

bro is a statistician and cannot do math apparently



— https://nitter.net/Annie_Wu_22/status/2034302404397588712#m
Not a huge impact from Iran yet, but a new net low in our Trump approval tracking today fwiw.
There's plenty of appetite for progressivism, etc., within the Democratic electorate, but the Very Online version of it doesn't really travel well.
These numbers will continue to update once games are played: natesilver.net/p/2026-march-…

Since the First Four is about to tip (*we* consider that part of the tournament even if your office pool doesn't), here are our final pre-tournament forecasts with updated injury info. Very close at the top.
natesilver.net/p/the-taco-tr…
Wrote about the game theory of the TACO trade. Which gets a lot more complicated when you're facing something like war in the Middle East.
Latest projections here: natesilver.net/p/2026-march-…

🏀 Took a deeper look at injury adjustments in our NCAA model tonight and found they were probably too aggressive. We'd always rather make the best projections we can. Michigan and Duke, which have more injuries, are helped by this, creating a ~3-way tie up top.

More analysis and all our numbers here. And you can find a link to our men's projections too, which we published late last night ICYMI.

natesilver.net/p/2026-womens…

The #1s are typically more dominant in women's hoops than for the men, but that's especially true this year. Some very, very good programs at the top. Big step down after that, except to some extent LSU which is kind of an unfair matchup for UCLA in the Elite Eight.

Our women's March Madness projections are ready! 🏀🏀🏀

1. UConn 35%
2. Texas 22%
3. South Carolina 21%
4. UCLA 17%
5. LSU 4%

The difference between public perceptions and gambler perceptions is funny sometimes. Miami (OH) is 31-1, widely perceived to have been robbed of a higher seed, but 7½-point underdogs in their First Four game against SMU according to Vegas. Our model has it a *bit* closer FWIW.

Will Iran break MAGA? (by Eli McKown-Dawson). So far, there’s not much erosion in Trump’s polling among Republicans. But elite opinion can trickle down to the party base.

Gas prices are set to go vertical. It's not exactly a bold prediction to suggest this will be bad for Trump's political standing.

War isn't what it once was. Past precedents are less useful than you'd think when predicting a war's political impact. But Iran still poses downside risks for Trump.
